request.question: Expressing a heterologous xylose isomerase from Piromyces sp. in Saccharomyces cerevisiae [CEN.PK](http://CEN.PK) will enable fermentation of xylose to ethanol at a yield of at least 0.4 g ethanol/g xylose within 48 hours under anaerobic conditions, compared to zero ethanol production in the parental strain, due to the enzyme bypassing the native redox-imbalanced xylose reductase/xylitol dehydrogenase pathway.

hypothesis.intervention: Expressing a heterologous xylose isomerase from Piromyces sp. in Saccharomyces cerevisiae CEN.PK
hypothesis.threshold: at least 0.4 g ethanol/g xylose within 48 hours
hypothesis.mechanistic_rationale: the enzyme bypassing the native redox-imbalanced xylose reductase/xylitol dehydrogenase pathway
hypothesis.readiness_rationale: The intervention, biological system, threshold, and mechanistic rationale are stated, but the measurable outcome lacks a named quantification assay/method. The comparator is stated as the parental strain but would benefit from exact strain/control designation before CRO execution.
clarifying_questions: What specific assay/analytical method will be used to quantify ethanol and xylose (e.g., HPLC, GC, enzymatic ethanol assay)?
What exact parental strain/control designation will be used for comparison?
hypothesis.literature_search_hint: Piromyces xylose isomerase Saccharomyces cerevisiae CEN.PK ethanol xylose fermentation
hypothesis.original_hypothesis: Expressing a heterologous xylose isomerase from Piromyces sp. in Saccharomyces cerevisiae [CEN.PK](http://CEN.PK) will enable fermentation of xylose to ethanol at a yield of at least 0.4 g ethanol/g xylose within 48 hours under anaerobic conditions, compared to zero ethanol production in the parental strain, due to the enzyme bypassing the native redox-imbalanced xylose reductase/xylitol dehydrogenase pathway.

references.title: High-level functional expression of a fungal xylose isomerase: the key to efficient ethanolic fermentation of xylose by ?
references.relevance_note: This paper directly reports functional expression of the Piromyces sp. XylA xylose isomerase in Saccharomyces cerevisiae for xylose/ethanolic fermentation, matching the intervention and yeast metabolic-engineering fermentation context of the hypothesis.

references.title: Chemical and Synthetic Genetic Array Analysis Identifies Genes that Suppress Xylose Utilization and Fermentation in Saccharomyces cerevisiae
references.relevance_note: This paper concerns engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae xylose utilization and fermentation, and its abstract notes common engineering by xylose isomerase; it is relevant background in the same organism/assay space but is not as direct for Piromyces XylA expression.

references.title: Directed evolution and secretory expression of xylose isomerase for improved utilisation of xylose in Saccharomyces cerevisiae
references.relevance_note: This paper studies recombinant Saccharomyces cerevisiae expressing xylose isomerase for improved xylose utilization, using a closely related intervention/fermentation context, though not necessarily the Piromyces sp. XI in CEN.PK.

literature_qc.explanation: On the intervention dimension, paper [0] explicitly describes functional expression of the Piromyces sp. XylA xylose isomerase, which is the same heterologous XI intervention in the hypothesis. On the biological-system dimension, [0] is in Saccharomyces cerevisiae; while the truncated abstract/title does not explicitly show CEN.PK, the system is highly similar and within the same yeast metabolic-engineering context. On the experiment-type dimension, [0] addresses efficient ethanolic fermentation of xylose, matching yeast metabolic engineering/fermentation; papers [1] and [3] provide additional related S. cerevisiae xylose-fermentation prior art.
literature_qc.recommended_action: A paper matching this experiment was found in the scientific literature. Review it before proceeding. If this is intentional replication, state that explicitly. If not, differentiate your approach.

protocol_candidates.protocol_name: Standard yeast_metabolic_engineering_fermentation protocol for expressing a heterologous xylose isomerase from piromyces sp. in saccharomyces cerevisiae cen.pk
protocol_candidates.adaptation_notes: Stub mode — no live protocols.io search performed. This is a placeholder candidate.
limitations: Stub output only — real protocols.io search requires PROTOCOLS_IO_TOKEN.

evidence_claims.claim: Post-thaw viability directly tests the stated biological outcome.
evidence_claims.support_summary: Outcome is explicitly present in user hypothesis.

evidence_claims.claim: Trehalose membrane stabilization rationale supports intervention plausibility.
evidence_claims.support_summary: Mechanistic rationale is explicitly stated in user input.

evidence_claims.claim: Expected +15 percentage-point threshold may be optimistic without pilot calibration.
evidence_claims.support_summary: Effect size is user-defined but not validated in this run.

risks.description: Hypothesis is underspecified: The intervention, biological system, threshold, and mechanistic rationale are stated, but the measurable outcome lacks a named quantification assay/method. The comparator is stated as the parental strain but would benefit from exact strain/control designation before CRO execution.. Missing required fields must be resolved before execution.
risks.mitigation: Clarify the following fields before proceeding: measurable_outcome

plan.objective: Determine whether Saccharomyces cerevisiae CEN.PK expressing Piromyces sp. xylose isomerase produces ethanol from xylose at ≥0.4 g ethanol/g xylose within 48 h under oxygen-limited batch fermentation.
plan.experimental_design: Construct or recover a CEN.PK derivative carrying a TEF1-promoter Piromyces sp. xylA expression cassette and compare it with parental CEN.PK carrying the matched empty vector in biological triplicate 50 mL oxygen-limited fermentations containing 20 g/L xylose. Xylose consumption and ethanol production are quantified by HPLC over 0–48 h, with glucose fermentation as a positive fermentation-capacity control and sterile medium as an abiotic background control.
controls: Parental Saccharomyces cerevisiae CEN.PK carrying pRS426-URA3 grown in YP medium containing 20 g/L D-glucose under the same oxygen-limited 30 °C fermentation format, expected to yield ≥0.42 g ethanol/g glucose within 24 h.
Sterile YP medium containing 20 g/L D-xylose incubated in sealed 125 mL serum bottles at 30 °C for 48 h without yeast cells.

step_by_step_protocol.description:
DAY 0 (PREP) | 15 min | Setup
Book the New Brunswick Innova 44R incubator shaker at 30 °C and 120 rpm for 72 h for 24 sealed 125 mL serum bottles.
Expected outcome: Incubator availability confirmed for 72 h with temperature log tolerance ±0.5 °C.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 45 min | HPLC setup
Calibrate the Agilent 1260 Infinity II HPLC-RID at 35 °C using 0.005 M H2SO4 mobile phase at 0.6 mL/min for 30 min.
Expected outcome: RID baseline drift is <2 µRIU/min over 10 min.
Safety: Handle sulfuric acid with chemical splash goggles and nitrile gloves.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 30 min | Reagent preparation
Dissolve 10.0 g yeast extract, 20.0 g peptone, and 20.0 g D-xylose in ultrapure water to 1.000 L at 22 °C.
Expected outcome: Medium is visibly homogeneous with 20.0 g/L xylose and no undissolved solids.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 45 min | Reagent preparation
Sterilize the YP-xylose medium at 121 °C and 15 psi for 20 min in a Tuttnauer 3870EA autoclave.
Expected outcome: Autoclave cycle reaches 121 °C for ≥20 min with indicator tape color change.
Safety: Use heat-resistant gloves and allow bottle pressure to equilibrate before opening the autoclave.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 30 min | Reagent preparation
Dissolve 10.0 g yeast extract, 20.0 g peptone, and 20.0 g D-glucose in ultrapure water to 1.000 L at 22 °C.
Expected outcome: Medium is visibly homogeneous with 20.0 g/L glucose and no undissolved solids.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 45 min | Reagent preparation
Sterilize the YP-glucose medium at 121 °C and 15 psi for 20 min in a Tuttnauer 3870EA autoclave.
Expected outcome: Autoclave cycle reaches 121 °C for ≥20 min with indicator tape color change.
Safety: Use heat-resistant gloves and allow bottle pressure to equilibrate before opening the autoclave.

DAY 1 | 30 min active, 48 h incubation | Strain recovery
Streak Saccharomyces cerevisiae CEN.PK pRS426-empty and CEN.PK pRS426-TEF1-Piromyces-xylA from −80 °C glycerol stocks onto SC-ura agar plates at 30 °C for 48 h.
Expected outcome: Each plate contains ≥30 isolated colonies after 48 h with colony diameter 1–3 mm.
Safety: Use BSL-1 aseptic handling and disinfect bench surfaces with 70% v/v ethanol.

DAY 3 | 20 min active, 16 h incubation | Seed culture
Inoculate three 5 mL SC-ura glucose cultures for each strain with single colonies at 30 °C and 200 rpm for 16 h.
Expected outcome: Six cultures reach OD600 2.0–6.0 after 16 h.
Safety: Use BSL-1 aseptic handling and tighten tube caps only to the first stop for gas exchange.

DAY 4 | 20 min | Seed culture QC
Measure OD600 of each 16 h seed culture using 200 µL culture per well in a BioTek Synergy H1 plate reader at 30 °C.
Expected outcome: Blank-corrected OD600 values fall between 2.0 and 6.0 with technical CV <10%.

DAY 4 | 30 min active, 12 h incubation | Xylose pre-adaptation
Transfer 1.0 mL of each seed culture into 49.0 mL YP-xylose medium in 250 mL baffled flasks at 30 °C and 200 rpm for 12 h.
Expected outcome: Pre-adaptation cultures reach OD600 1.0–3.0 after 12 h on xylose medium.
Safety: Use BSL-1 aseptic handling and keep flasks covered with sterile vented closures.

DAY 4 | 45 min | Fermentation setup
Dispense 50.0 mL sterile YP-xylose medium into each of 21 sterile 125 mL serum bottles at 22 °C.
Expected outcome: Twenty-one bottles contain 50.0 ± 0.5 mL medium by gravimetric check.
Safety: Use aseptic technique in a Thermo Scientific 1300 Series A2 biosafety cabinet.

DAY 4 | 10 min | Fermentation setup
Dispense 50.0 mL sterile YP-glucose medium into each of 3 sterile 125 mL serum bottles at 22 °C.
Expected outcome: Three bottles contain 50.0 ± 0.5 mL medium by gravimetric check.
Safety: Use aseptic technique in a Thermo Scientific 1300 Series A2 biosafety cabinet.

DAY 4 | 2 h | Fermentation setup
Sparge each filled serum bottle with sterile nitrogen gas at 100 mL/min for 5 min at 22 °C.
Expected outcome: Dissolved oxygen falls below 0.5 mg/L in one representative bottle measured immediately after sparging.
Safety: Nitrogen asphyxiation risk; operate in a ventilated room with an oxygen monitor reading >19.5% O2.

DAY 4 | 45 min | Fermentation setup
Seal each serum bottle with a sterile butyl rubber stopper and aluminum crimp at 22 °C.
Expected outcome: Each bottle remains sealed with no bubble formation during a 10 s inversion leak check.
Safety: Use eye protection during crimping to prevent injury from mis-seated caps.

DAY 5 | 20 min | Inoculum QC
Measure OD600 of each 12 h xylose pre-adaptation culture using 200 µL culture per well in a BioTek Synergy H1 plate reader at 30 °C.
Expected outcome: Blank-corrected OD600 values are 1.0–3.0 with technical CV <10%.

DAY 5 | 15 min | Inoculum preparation
Centrifuge the xylose pre-adaptation cultures at 3,000 × g for 5 min at 22 °C in an Eppendorf 5810 R centrifuge.
Expected outcome: Each tube contains a visible yeast pellet ≥20 mg wet mass.
Safety: Balance centrifuge buckets within 0.1 g.

DAY 5 | 30 min | Inoculum preparation
Resuspend each yeast pellet in sterile 0.9% w/v NaCl to OD600 20.0 at 22 °C.
Expected outcome: Resuspended inocula measure OD600 20.0 ± 1.0 after 1:20 dilution in 0.9% w/v NaCl.
Safety: Use BSL-1 aseptic handling in a biosafety cabinet.

DAY 5 | 45 min | Fermentation inoculation
Inject 2.5 mL OD600 20.0 inoculum into each assigned sealed 50.0 mL YP-xylose serum bottle using a sterile 5 mL syringe at 22 °C.
Expected outcome: Fermentation bottles start at OD600 1.0 ± 0.1 in 52.5 mL total volume.
Safety: Use sharps container disposal immediately after each needle puncture.

DAY 5 | 10 min | Fermentation inoculation
Inject 2.5 mL OD600 20.0 parental pRS426-empty inoculum into each assigned sealed 50.0 mL YP-glucose serum bottle using a sterile 5 mL syringe at 22 °C.
Expected outcome: Positive-control glucose bottles start at OD600 1.0 ± 0.1 in 52.5 mL total volume.
Safety: Use sharps container disposal immediately after each needle puncture.

DAY 5 | 48 h | Batch fermentation
Incubate all inoculated and sterile serum bottles at 30 °C and 120 rpm for 48 h in the New Brunswick Innova 44R incubator shaker.
Expected outcome: Temperature log remains 30.0 ± 0.5 °C and agitation remains 120 ± 5 rpm for 48 h.
Safety: Confirm bottle crimps are secure before shaking to reduce leak risk.

DAY 5 | 45 min | Sampling
Withdraw 1.0 mL fermentation broth from each bottle at 0 h using a sterile 1 mL syringe at 22 °C.
Expected outcome: Each 0 h sample tube contains 1.0 ± 0.05 mL broth with no visible stopper fragments.
Safety: Use sharps container disposal immediately after each needle puncture.

DAY 6 | 45 min | Sampling
Withdraw 1.0 mL fermentation broth from each bottle at 24 h using a sterile 1 mL syringe at 22 °C.
Expected outcome: Each 24 h sample tube contains 1.0 ± 0.05 mL broth with no visible stopper fragments.
Safety: Use sharps container disposal immediately after each needle puncture.

DAY 7 | 45 min | Sampling
Withdraw 1.0 mL fermentation broth from each bottle at 48 h using a sterile 1 mL syringe at 22 °C.
Expected outcome: Each 48 h sample tube contains 1.0 ± 0.05 mL broth with no visible stopper fragments.
Safety: Use sharps container disposal immediately after each needle puncture.

DAY 7 | 30 min | Sample clarification
Centrifuge each 1.0 mL broth sample at 16,000 × g for 5 min at 4 °C in an Eppendorf 5424 R centrifuge.
Expected outcome: Each sample forms a compact yeast pellet with clarified supernatant turbidity OD600 <0.05 after 1:10 dilution.
Safety: Balance microcentrifuge tubes within 0.01 g.

DAY 7 | 1 h | Sample clarification
Filter 700 µL clarified supernatant from each sample through a 0.22 µm PVDF syringe filter into an HPLC vial at 22 °C.
Expected outcome: Each HPLC vial contains ≥600 µL particle-free filtrate with no visible pellet carryover.
Safety: Dispose of filters and syringes as BSL-1 biological waste.

DAY 7 | 12 h | HPLC analysis
Inject 10 µL of each standard and filtered fermentation sample onto the Agilent 1260 Infinity II HPLC-RID at 35 °C with 0.005 M H2SO4 at 0.6 mL/min for 30 min per injection.
Expected outcome: External-standard calibration R² is ≥0.995 for xylose, glucose, ethanol, xylitol, glycerol, and acetate from 0.1–25 g/L.
Safety: Handle HPLC vials containing ethanol away from ignition sources.

DAY 8 | 3 h | Data analysis and interpretation
Quantify ethanol yield in R 4.3.2 using g ethanol produced per g xylose consumed at 48 h, Welch two-sample t-test for engineered versus parental yield, Benjamini-Hochberg correction for byproduct comparisons, and one-sample t-test versus 0.40 g/g threshold at α=0.05.
Expected outcome: Decision table reports mean engineered ethanol yield, 95% CI, p-values, residual xylose g/L, and pass/fail status against ≥0.40 g/g xylose within 48 h.

assumptions: CEN.PK auxotrophic markers are compatible with the selected pRS426-TEF1-Piromyces-xylA-URA3 plasmid and pRS426-URA3 empty-vector comparator.
Piromyces sp. XylA is codon-compatible or codon-optimized for yeast and expressed as a soluble cytosolic enzyme under the TEF1 promoter.
Anaerobic or oxygen-limited 20 g/L xylose medium with 10 g/L yeast extract and 20 g/L peptone is sufficient to reveal ethanol formation differences within 48 h.
HPLC refractive-index detection resolves xylose, xylitol, glycerol, acetate, and ethanol with calibration R² ≥0.995 over the expected concentration range.
decision_criteria: Proceed to scale-up if the Piromyces xylA strain produces ≥0.40 g ethanol/g xylose by 48 h and consumes ≥70% of the initial 20 g/L xylose in at least 2 of 3 biological replicates.
Run a promoter-copy-number pilot if ethanol yield is 0.20–0.39 g/g xylose or residual xylose remains >10 g/L at 48 h despite confirmed xylA transcript abundance ≥5-fold above parental background.
Stop the fermentation claim if engineered and parental xylose cultures differ by <0.05 g ethanol/g xylose at 48 h with Welch t-test p≥0.05 and HPLC calibration R² ≥0.995.
reproducibility_notes: Use the same CEN.PK strain background, passage number ≤5 from glycerol stock, and verified URA3 selection status for all biological replicates.
Record plasmid lot, xylA insert sequence version, promoter sequence, copy-number format, and transformation batch for every engineered colony.
Use one batch of YP-xylose medium and one batch of YP-glucose medium for all replicate fermentations.
Calibrate the Agilent 1260 Infinity II HPLC-RID on the same day as sample injection using freshly weighed external standards.
Standardise bottle headspace ratio at 50 mL liquid in 125 mL serum bottles and agitation at 120 rpm in the same incubator shaker.
feedback_incorporated: Reagent Preparation › materials (rating 5/5): something is off

materials.item_name: New Brunswick Innova 44R refrigerated incubator shaker use
materials.quantity: 72 h booking for 24 sealed 125 mL bottles
materials.uncertainty_note: Estimated as internal academic equipment recharge; actual cost depends on departmental recharge policy.

materials.item_name: Agilent 1260 Infinity II HPLC with RID use
materials.quantity: 1 extended run including calibration and ~12 h analysis
materials.uncertainty_note: Assumes an internal daily/overnight instrument fee and does not include service contract costs.

materials.item_name: Organic acid/carbohydrate HPLC column compatible with 0.005 M H2SO4 and RID (e.g., HPX-87H/Hi-Plex H) amortized use
materials.uncertainty_note: If a new column must be purchased, budget may increase by about €800–1,200 plus guard column.
Guard cartridge consumption varies strongly with sample cleanliness and institutional practice.
Internal autoclave recharges are often not billed directly but energy and maintenance costs are real.

materials.uncertainty_note: Cost is an estimated facility recharge including certification amortization.

materials.uncertainty_note: Some core facilities bill per hour rather than per session.

Covers pre-adaptation harvests and saline resuspension handling with spares.

line_items.item_name: Sterile 0.22 µm gas filters for nitrogen sparging line
line_items.quantity: 24 single-use filters or equivalent sterile setup
line_items.uncertainty_note: A validated reusable sterile gas manifold could reduce this cost.

line_items.quantity: ~2 m used for media sterilization cycles
line_items.uncertainty_note: Allocated cost from one roll.

line_items.item_name: Mixed sterile pipette tips (10, 200 and 1000 µL)
line_items.uncertainty_note: Filter tips would raise the cost to roughly €0.08–0.15 per tip.
Assumes bulk academic pricing.
Glove costs fluctuate with institutional contracts but are well bounded.

line_items.quantity: 1 small container or equivalent capacity for ~120 sharps
line_items.uncertainty_note: Disposal fees may be charged centrally rather than per experiment.
Amortized PPE use; purchase price is typically €10–20.
Amortized PPE use; purchase price is typically €40–80.
Exact price depends strongly on microbiology-grade brand and pack size.
Academic pricing varies by supplier and whether casein/soy peptone is specified.

line_items.quantity: 100 g bottle (uses ~30 g including pre-adaptation and fermentation medium)
line_items.uncertainty_note: Protocol states 1 L YP-xylose but total downstream use exceeds 1 L, so extra medium/reagent margin is included.

line_items.quantity: 500 g bottle (uses ~25 g for YP-glucose plus SC-ura)
line_items.uncertainty_note: Commodity reagent with stable bulk academic pricing.
Cost reflects consumables/resin allocation rather than purchased bottled water.

line_items.item_name: Sulfuric acid for 0.005 M H2SO4 HPLC mobile phase
line_items.uncertainty_note: Purchase quantity greatly exceeds experimental consumption but is the practical minimum pack size.

line_items.item_name: HPLC-grade water for mobile phase and standards
line_items.uncertainty_note: If in-house LC-MS grade water is available, direct purchase cost may be lower.

line_items.item_name: Yeast nitrogen base without amino acids
line_items.uncertainty_note: Selective media formulation may require verification of ammonium sulfate inclusion.
Dropout mix price varies by formulation and supplier.
Commercial prepared plates can replace this and reduce hands-on preparation.

line_items.item_name: Ammonium sulfate for SC-ura medium if not included in YNB
line_items.uncertainty_note: Some YNB formulations already contain ammonium sulfate, so verify before ordering.

line_items.item_name: SC-ura glucose liquid medium components for seed cultures
line_items.uncertainty_note: Cost allocated from bulk YNB/dropout/glucose stocks because only ~30 mL is consumed.

line_items.item_name: Sodium chloride for sterile 0.9% w/v saline
line_items.uncertainty_note: Commodity reagent; minimum pack size dominates cost.
Can be prepared in-house from absolute ethanol at similar cost.

line_items.item_name: Nitrogen gas cylinder usage for sparging
line_items.quantity: ~20 L gas used; charged as cylinder/day allocation
line_items.uncertainty_note: Cylinder rental and delivery fees may exceed gas consumption cost.

line_items.item_name: D-xylose analytical standard for HPLC calibration
line_items.uncertainty_note: Standard purity grade and certificate requirements affect price.

line_items.item_name: D-glucose analytical standard for HPLC calibration
line_items.uncertainty_note: Commodity standard; certified reference material would be more expensive.

line_items.item_name: Ethanol HPLC calibration standard or HPLC-grade ethanol
line_items.uncertainty_note: Denatured ethanol is unsuitable for calibration; verify analytical grade.
Price varies with purity and pack size.
Commodity calibration compound; certified standards cost more.

line_items.item_name: Acetic acid or sodium acetate analytical standard for acetate calibration
line_items.uncertainty_note: Select the same chemical form used to prepare calibration curve and report acetate equivalents.
Open-source software; no license fee expected.

line_items.item_name: Saccharomyces cerevisiae CEN.PK pRS426-empty glycerol stock (parental/empty-vector negative control strain)
line_items.uncertainty_note: Assumes existing or MTA stock with internal handling/verification cost; new acquisition and sequence verification could be higher.

line_items.item_name: Saccharomyces cerevisiae CEN.PK pRS426-TEF1-Piromyces-xylA glycerol stock (engineered test strain)
line_items.uncertainty_note: Protocol begins from an existing stock; de novo strain construction is not costed and would be much more expensive.

line_items.item_name: YP-glucose positive-control fermentation condition with parental pRS426-empty inoculum
line_items.uncertainty_note: Cost is the incremental allocated cost of control bottles/media beyond shared materials.

line_items.quantity: 3 sealed serum bottles assumed within 21 xylose bottles
line_items.uncertainty_note: The protocol refers to inoculated and sterile bottles but does not explicitly state the sterile-control count; three bottles are assumed.

line_items.item_name: External calibration standards covering xylose, glucose, ethanol, xylitol, glycerol and acetate
line_items.uncertainty_note: This is the allocated preparation cost; bulk standard chemicals are itemized separately.

uncertainty_notes: The protocol prepares only 1.0 L YP-xylose but downstream steps require at least ~1.35 L before losses; the estimate includes extra xylose/YP capacity to make sufficient medium.
Major cost uncertainty comes from whether strains, HPLC column, serum bottles and shared instruments are already available or must be newly purchased/recharged.
Consumable counts assume 24 bottles sampled at 3 time points, one sterile syringe/needle/filter path per sample or puncture to minimize contamination and carryover.

phases.phase_name: Day 0: Facility booking, HPLC readiness, and media preparation/sterilization
bottlenecks: Innova 44R shaker availability for uninterrupted 72 h block
Agilent 1260 HPLC-RID baseline stability and column equilibration
Autoclave queue and cooling time for 1 L media bottles
Availability of sterile serum bottles, butyl stoppers, crimps, nitrogen line, and SC-ura agar plates

phases.phase_name: Day 1-3: Yeast strain recovery from glycerol stocks on SC-ura agar
dependencies: Day 0: Facility booking, HPLC readiness, and media preparation/sterilization
bottlenecks: Slow colony recovery from -80 °C stocks
Contamination or poor isolation requiring restreaking
Incubator temperature excursions over 48 h

phases.phase_name: Day 3-4: Overnight seed cultures in SC-ura glucose
dependencies: Day 1-3: Yeast strain recovery from glycerol stocks on SC-ura agar
bottlenecks: Colonies not sufficiently isolated or wrong colony morphology
Seed cultures outside OD600 2.0-6.0 after 16 h
Shaker capacity at 30 °C/200 rpm

phases.phase_name: Day 4: Seed OD QC, xylose pre-adaptation setup, and sealed serum bottle preparation
dependencies: Day 3-4: Overnight seed cultures in SC-ura glucose
bottlenecks: Plate reader scheduling for OD600 measurements
Biosafety cabinet availability for dispensing and inoculations
Nitrogen gas regulator/sterile filter availability and oxygen monitor clearance
Manual bottleneck from sparging 24 bottles and crimp-sealing without leaks

dependencies: Day 4: Seed OD QC, xylose pre-adaptation setup, and sealed serum bottle preparation
bottlenecks: Pre-adaptation cultures failing to reach OD600 1.0-3.0
Strain-dependent lag on xylose medium
Shaker/incubator space shared with bottle preparation timing

phases.phase_name: Day 5: Inoculum QC, biomass harvest/resuspension, fermentation inoculation, and 0 h sampling
bottlenecks: Centrifuge rotor availability for multiple culture tubes
Accuracy of OD600 normalization to 20.0 ± 1.0
Needle puncture and crimp integrity during inoculation/sampling
Time-critical coordination so 0 h samples are collected immediately after inoculation

phases.phase_name: Day 5-7: 48 h anaerobic batch fermentation with 24 h and 48 h sampling
dependencies: Day 5: Inoculum QC, biomass harvest/resuspension, fermentation inoculation, and 0 h sampling
bottlenecks: Innova 44R shaker temperature/agitation stability for full 48 h
Serum bottle leaks or stopper coring during syringe sampling
Sampling must occur close to 24 h and 48 h timepoints
Biological variability in xylose fermentation rate and ethanol production

phases.phase_name: Day 7: Sample clarification and HPLC vial preparation
dependencies: Day 5-7: 48 h anaerobic batch fermentation with 24 h and 48 h sampling
bottlenecks: Microcentrifuge capacity for 72 samples plus standards/QC
Filter clogging from incomplete clarification or pellet carryover
Sample labeling and chain-of-custody errors across three timepoints and 24 bottles

phases.phase_name: Day 7-8: HPLC-RID run for standards and fermentation samples
dependencies: Day 7: Sample clarification and HPLC vial preparation
bottlenecks: HPLC autosampler availability for an overnight 12 h sequence
RID baseline drift or column pressure instability
Carryover or poor calibration linearity requiring standard reinjection
Power interruption or autosampler vial misplacement during overnight run

phases.phase_name: Day 8: Data analysis, statistics, interpretation, and results write-up
dependencies: Day 7-8: HPLC-RID run for standards and fermentation samples
bottlenecks: Chromatographic peak overlap for ethanol/xylitol/glycerol/acetate requiring manual integration review
Outlier replicate bottles or contamination controls complicating statistics
Need to verify dilution factors, bottle volumes, and xylose consumption calculations before final decision table

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: at least 0.4 g ethanol/g xylose within 48 hours improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.

cro_ready_brief.objective: Determine whether Saccharomyces cerevisiae CEN.PK expressing Piromyces sp. xylose isomerase produces ethanol from xylose at ≥0.4 g ethanol/g xylose within 48 h under oxygen-limited batch fermentation.
scope_of_work: Execute pilot-first HeLa cryopreservation comparison between DMSO baseline and trehalose arm.
Deliver replicate-confirmed viability analysis and mitigation traceability report.
cro_ready_brief.sample_count: At least 3 biological replicates per arm
cro_ready_brief.materials_responsibility: CRO to quote and confirm all materials before ordering.
questions_for_cro: Can you support controlled-rate freezing logs in each run?
Can you include replicate-level batch metadata in the final report?
